zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert le Strange於1448年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托時，他的父親John le Strange52歲，母親Mrs. Strange52歲。他於1460年與Elizabeth Norton在英國格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1489年在英國布里斯托被列為mayor of bristol。他去世於1491年在英國布里斯托享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert le Strange于1448年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托时，他的父亲John le Strange52岁，母亲Mrs. Strange52岁。他于1460年与Elizabeth Norton在英国格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1489年在英国布里斯托被列为mayor of bristol。他去世于1491年在英国布里斯托享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert le Strange was born in 1448, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, his father, John le Strange, was 52 and his mother, Mrs. Strange, was 52. He married Elizabeth Norton in 1460, in Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. In 1489, at the age of 41, his occupation is listed as mayor of bristol in Bristol, England. He died in 1491, in Bristol, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert le Strange est né en 1448, à Bristol, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, John le Strange, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Strange, avait 52 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Norton en 1460, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. En 1489, à l’âge de 41 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mayor of bristol à Bristol, Angleterre. Il est décédé en 1491, à Bristol, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert le Strange 1448 in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John le Strange 52 und seine Mutter Mrs. Strange 52. Er hat 1460 in Gloucestershire, England Elizabeth Norton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1489, als er 41 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mayor of bristol in Bristol, England aufgeführt. Er starb 1491 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Bristol, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert le Strange è nato il nel 1448 nel Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, John le Strange, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Strange, aveva 52 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Norton nel 1460, nel Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1489, all'età di 41 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mayor of bristol nel Bristol, England. Morì nel 1491, nel Bristol, England, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert le Strangeが1448年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルで生まれた時，父John le Strangeは52歳，母Mrs. Strangeは52歳であった。1460年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでElizabeth Nortonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1489年，41歳の時，イギリスブリストルでの職業はmayor of bristolと記録されている。彼は1491年にイギリスブリストルで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert le Strange(이)가 1448년 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John le Strange(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Strange(은)는 52세였다. 그는 1460년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 Elizabeth Norton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1489년, 41세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 브리스톨에서 mayor of bristol이었다. 그는 1491년 영국 브리스톨에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Osbaldeston, Lancashire, England, United: Osbaldeston, Lancashire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert le Strange nasceu em 1448, em Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John le Strange, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Strange, tinha 52 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Norton em 1460, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1489, com 41 anos, sua profissão aparece como mayor of bristol em Bristol, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 1491, em Bristol, Inglaterra, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert le Strange родился в 1448 г. в местности бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени John le Strange было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Strange, 52. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Norton в 1460 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. В 1489 году, в возрасте 41 год,  он работал по профессии mayor of bristol в местности бристол, Англия. Он умер в 1491 г. в местности бристол, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert le Strange nació en 1448, en Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, John le Strange, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Mrs. Strange, tenía 52 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Norton en 1460, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1489, a la edad de 41 años, su ocupación registrada era mayor of bristol en Bristol, Inglaterra. Murió en 1491, en Bristol, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años.
